parts.text:
Investigate prior-session provenance for the question: "Why does src/auth/queue.ts deduplicate refresh work per tenant instead of using one global mutex?" Expected relevant prior-session title: "Auth refresh queue rationale". Ignore billing retry and markdown parser distractor sessions.

Required process: first use session_lookup, then session_detail with detail='messages', then message_detail for one exact message before answering. Do not load all transcripts. Return only the exact evidence you found and a concise answer, including session_id, blob_id, and message_id. If unavailable, use empty strings.
I’ll look up the prior session by the expected title, inspect only message summaries, then fetch one exact relevant message for provenance.

parts.text:
session_id: `ses_1e4f57505ffej7UsSdlKnpBXHb`  
blob_id: `auth_queue`  
message_id: `msg_e1b0a8b08001z9DLoLG9Pvuk4c`

Evidence: “same-tenant duplicate refresh requests should coalesce into one refresh, while different tenants should continue in parallel.”

Answer: `src/auth/queue.ts` deduplicates per tenant because a global mutex would make unrelated tenants block each other. Same-tenant refreshes should collapse into one in-flight refresh, but different tenants must run concurrently.
